# flashblade_network_interface (Data Source)

Reads an existing FlashBlade network interface by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "flashblade_network_interface" "example" {
  name = "existing-vip"
}

output "network_interface_address" {
  value = data.flashblade_network_interface.example.address
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the network interface to look up.

### Read-Only

- `address` (String) The IPv4 address of the network interface.
- `attached_servers` (List of String) List of server names attached to this interface.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the network interface is enabled.
- `gateway` (String) The gateway address for this network interface.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the network interface.
- `mtu` (Number) Maximum transmission unit (MTU) in bytes.
- `netmask` (String) The subnet mask for this network interface.
- `realms` (List of String) List of realms associated with this network interface.
- `services` (String) The service type for this network interface (data, sts, egress-only, replication).
- `subnet_name` (String) The name of the subnet this interface is attached to.
- `type` (String) The network interface type (e.g. vip).
- `vlan` (Number) VLAN ID. 0 means untagged.
